allowed_claims: STWM improves internal baselines and legacysem on the official setting.
STWM provides complementary continuity-heavy trace-belief advantages on this item-aligned diagnostic protocol.
STWM significantly outperforms CoTracker on long-gap and occlusion/reappearance continuity-heavy subsets.
SAM2 remains the strongest external baseline overall on the 389-item hard-case diagnostic protocol.
forbidden_claims: 389-item diagnostic protocol is a full video benchmark.
External baseline failure proves STWM superiority.
main_paper_wording: On the 389-item hard-case diagnostic protocol, SAM2 is the strongest external baseline overall and CoTracker also exceeds STWM in aggregate top-1 accuracy. STWM should therefore not be presented as an external overall SOTA tracker; instead, the key result is complementary: its trace-conditioned belief readout significantly improves over CoTracker on long-gap and occlusion/reappearance reacquisition subsets, while SAM2 remains stronger overall and on OOD/crossing-heavy cases. This boundary clarifies STWM as a future identity association and belief mechanism, not a replacement for general-purpose video segmentation or point tracking.

reviewer_risk_notes.Is STWM a tracker?: No. The paper should frame STWM as a trace-conditioned future identity association and belief readout, evaluated against trackers as strong external references.
reviewer_risk_notes.Is this full benchmark or diagnostic pro: It is a 389-item hard-case diagnostic item-aligned protocol with local 16-frame windows and single future-frame candidate association, not a full-video tracking benchmark.
reviewer_risk_notes.Why not just CoTracker?: CoTracker is stronger overall, but STWM significantly improves over CoTracker on long-gap and occlusion/reappearance continuity subsets, which are central to future identity association.
reviewer_risk_notes.Why not just SAM2?: SAM2 is stronger overall and should be acknowledged as such; STWM contributes complementary trace-belief continuity evidence rather than universal tracking dominance.
